# Verification Checklist: {skill-name}

**Generated by:** Stage 2 (Prep / handoff-sesh)
**Date:** {date}
**Design doc:** `design/design-doc.md`
**Used by:** Stage 3 (Build / build-sesh) during testing, Stage 4 (Release / qa-sesh) for QA template generation

---

## How to Use This Checklist

This checklist is generated from the design doc. It is not a test script — it is a catalogue of failure modes to watch for during testing. For each step in the skill:

- **Gate compliance** — did Claude stop when it was supposed to?
- **Step skipping** — did Claude decide this step wasn't needed and move past it?
- **Step merging** — did Claude combine two distinct steps into one?
- **File hallucination** — did Claude describe saving a file without actually saving it?
- **Reference drift** — did Claude use its own knowledge instead of reading the reference file?
- **Output format deviation** — did Claude produce output in the wrong format or structure?
- **Artifact launch compliance** — did Claude launch the artifact correctly (side panel vs. inline vs. file)?

Mark each item PASS, FAIL, or N/A. If FAIL, note the specific behavior observed.

---

## Step-by-Step Checks

> **Instructions for handoff-sesh when populating this template:**
> Replace each `{...}` placeholder with content derived from the design doc.
> Add one section per step in the skill. Remove this instruction block before saving.

---

### Step {N}: {Step Name}

**Gate compliance**
- Gate present in this step: {Yes / No}
- Gate strength level: {1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / N/A}
- Expected behavior: {what Claude should do — e.g., "stop and wait for user to type 'confirm'"}
- Blowthrough risk: {Low / Medium / High} — {brief reason, e.g., "step is at end of long output, easy to ignore"}
- [ ] PASS — Claude stopped as expected
- [ ] FAIL — Claude proceeded without waiting. Observed: ___
- [ ] N/A

**Step skipping risk**
- Skippable: {Yes / No}
- Why Claude might skip it: {e.g., "step produces a reference file Claude may decide isn't necessary"}
- [ ] PASS — Step executed as written
- [ ] FAIL — Step was skipped. Observed: ___
- [ ] N/A

**Step merging risk**
- Merge candidate: {Yes / No} — {e.g., "likely to merge with Step N+1 because both involve the same file"}
- [ ] PASS — Step executed as a distinct step
- [ ] FAIL — Step was merged with ___. Observed: ___
- [ ] N/A

**File hallucination check**
- File produced in this step: {Yes / No}
- Expected file: `{relative/path/filename.ext}` (or N/A)
- Verification method: Read the file back after the step completes. Confirm it exists and contains expected content.
- [ ] PASS — File exists and content is correct
- [ ] FAIL — File not found or content incorrect. Observed: ___
- [ ] N/A

**Reference drift check**
- Reference file used in this step: {Yes / No}
- Expected reference: `{relative/path/reference.md}` (or N/A)
- Drift signal: {e.g., "Claude used general knowledge about [topic] instead of reading the file"}
- [ ] PASS — Claude read the reference file before generating content
- [ ] FAIL — Claude used training knowledge instead. Observed: ___
- [ ] N/A

**Output format check**
- Specific format required: {Yes / No}
- Expected format: {e.g., "markdown table with columns: Step | Risk | Mitigation"}
- [ ] PASS — Format matches spec
- [ ] FAIL — Format deviated. Observed: ___
- [ ] N/A

**Artifact launch check**
- Artifact produced in this step: {Yes / No}
- Expected launch method: {side panel / downloadable file / inline / N/A}
- Launch instruction in SKILL.md: {quote the relevant instruction, or N/A}
- [ ] PASS — Artifact launched correctly
- [ ] FAIL — Artifact not launched / launched incorrectly. Observed: ___
- [ ] N/A

---

<!-- Repeat the above section for each step in the skill -->

---

## Cross-Cutting Checks

These checks apply across the full skill run, not to individual steps.

### Happy Path Completion
- [ ] PASS — All steps completed, all expected output files exist, no gates blown through
- [ ] FAIL — Describe: ___

### Friction Path Handling
- [ ] PASS — Skill handled non-standard user inputs at each step without breaking
- [ ] FAIL — Skill broke or stalled at step ___. Observed: ___

### Session Recovery
- [ ] PASS — If session was interrupted mid-skill, progressive saves allowed resumption
- [ ] FAIL — State was lost. Observed: ___
- [ ] N/A — Progressive save not applicable for this skill

### File Inventory
After a complete run, verify all expected output files exist:

| File | Expected Path | Exists? | Notes |
|------|--------------|---------|-------|
| {File name} | `{relative/path}` | [ ] Yes / [ ] No | |

<!-- Add one row per file the skill is expected to produce -->

---

## Failure Routing

If any FAIL is recorded:
- **Gate failures** → escalate gate language in SKILL.md (see `references/skill-writing-patterns.md` Section 1)
- **Step skip/merge failures** → add explicit step separation language; consider adding a soft prompt between steps
- **File hallucination** → add file verification instruction to the step; ensure path and filename are explicit
- **Reference drift** → move reference read instruction to the top of the step; add "Do not use training knowledge"
- **Format deviation** → add explicit format spec with an example inline in the step
- **Artifact launch failure** → check launch method against `references/skill-writing-patterns.md` Section 5

Route all FAIL items back to Stage 3 (build-sesh) with this checklist attached.
If 3 or more failure cycles occur on the same step, escalate to Stage 1 (design) — the step may be structurally ambiguous.
